title: The same information on multiple boards
excerpt:

posts.author: mbe (mb)
posts.cookedHtml:
I have a timeline in an subitems that needs to come up in another board as an item, too.
As it is a subitem, I cannot set an automation, that when the timeline in the subitem gets changed, the timeline in the item changes as well.
Also, I cannot use a mirror column as I need to work with timeline on both boards (seeing the sub-item-dates in the calendar widget and using the item’s timeline in automations such as “14 days befor date arrives, send an e-mail”).
What I need is a connection between two values that makes sure, the values are always the same.

posts.author: BruceGosk (Bruce Gosk)
posts.cookedHtml:
I received this today - you should try out the app.

posts.author: mbe (mb)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thanks for the idea, appreciate it!
Unfortunately, this is always kimited to one item and would mean that I have to create the automation for each item; having 100 items = needing the automation 100 times and always adding a new automation when adding a new item.
This is why the monday team suggested creating a feature request, as by now there is no automation for this case.

posts.author: BruceGosk (Bruce Gosk)
posts.cookedHtml: Have you tried creating that automation with the app? (it does read that way though that seems very silly to me - So I imagine you select ‘items’ on that board, not a specific item. Hope I’m correct ).

posts.author: LeanneM (Leanne McConnachie)
posts.cookedHtml: I have a ‘Speaker Board’ – basically, the mothership for all our speaker info. What I really wanted was for confirmed speakers to automatically appear as subitems in the right event board, without me having to manually copy everything. Turns out, a bunch of automations did the trick! If you’re dealing with speaker data across multiple events and want to know how I made this happen, let me know, and I’ll break down the automation flow.

posts.author: mbe (mb)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi Leanne, Thank you. It sounds pretty similar to me, yes. Do they update automatically? Meaning that, if you changed a date/timeline on one board, the other date changes too?

posts.author: mbe (mb)
posts.cookedHtml: Thank you fr your reply. I am not sure what you’re saying. When setting up the automation, I have to select the item the automation will apply to ([this item] in the template), otherwise it would not run. There is not “all items” option.

posts.author: BruceGosk (Bruce Gosk)
posts.cookedHtml:
